title: Pete Conrad '53, the third man on the moon - YouTube
content: Today marks 50 years since the moonwalk of Apollo 12 astronaut Pete Conrad '53, making him the third man to walk on the moon.

title: TIL there was a third astronaut with Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin ...
content: TIL there was a third astronaut with Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin on the Apollo 11 mission, Michael Collins, who stayed back on Apollo 11

title: Moonwalkers - NASA Science
content: NASA’s STORIE Mission to Tell Tale of Earth’s Ring Current. Astronaut in a white spacesuit standing on the surface of the Moon next to a robotic spacecraft with his human crew vehicle on the horizon. Neil Armstrong and Edwin "Buzz" Aldrin were the first of 12 human beings to walk on the Moon. Four of America's moonwalkers are still alive: Aldrin (Apollo 11), David Scott (Apollo 15), Charles Duke (Apollo 16), and Harrison Schmitt (Apollo 17). Three astronauts made the journey from Earth to the Moon twice: James Lovell (Apollo 8 and Apollo 13), John Young (Apollo 10 and Apollo 16), and Gene Cernan (Apollo 10 and Apollo 17). Headshot of man in a white space suit holding a helmet in front of a backdrop photo of the moon. NASA's Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO) was sent to the Moon to make high-resolution maps of the composition of the lunar…. NASA explores the unknown in air and space, innovates for the benefit of humanity, and inspires the world through discovery.

title: Michael Collins was the third member of the 1969 Apollo 11 crew ...
content: Remembering the 3rd Apollo 11 crew member Michael Collins (October 31, 1930 – April 28, 2021 American astronaut who flew the Apollo 11 command

title: The 12 men who walked on the moon
content: At left: Armstrong sits inside the lunar module after his historic walk on the surface of the moon. Image: NASA file image shows Buzz Aldrin on the moon next to the Lunar Module Eagle. Apollo 11 astronaut Buzz Aldrin, the second man to walk on the moon, stands next to the lunar module "Eagle"  on July 20, 1969. Apollo 11 from launch to lunar landing: Photos from the first journey to the moon. A young Buzz Aldrin poses in a NASA photograph at left. Charles "Pete" Conrad, the third man to walk on the moon, poses at left in 1965  before his first space flight aboard Gemini 5. Apollo 12 lunar module pilot Alan Bean poses in 1969 at right. Alan Bean sits by two of his paintings at an exhibition of his artwork at the National Air and Space Museum to celebrate the 40th anniversary of the first Apollo moon landing in 2009. The crew of the Apollo 14 lunar landing mission, from left, Stuart Roosa, commander Alan Shepard and lunar module pilot Edgar Mitchell.
